title.en: Docker for Python: Containerizing Your App

description.en: Learn Docker fundamentals — Dockerfile, images, containers, docker-compose, and containerizing Python web applications.
description.ta: Docker அடிப்படைகளைக் கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள் — Dockerfile, படங்கள், கொள்கலன்கள், docker-compose மற்றும் Python வலை பயன்பாடுகளை கொள்கலனாக்குதல்.

story_variants.en:
## The Shipping Container

Vaathiyaar shows a shipping container. "'It works on my machine' — the most dangerous phrase in software. **Docker** solves this by packaging your app, its dependencies, and its environment into a **container** that runs identically everywhere."

### Why Docker?
- **Consistency** — Same environment dev → staging → production
- **Isolation** — Each app gets its own container
- **Portability** — Runs on any machine with Docker
- **Reproducibility** — Build once, run anywhere
- **Scalability** — Spin up multiple containers easily

### Key Concepts

| Concept | What It Is | Analogy |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| **Image** | Read-only template | Blueprint/Recipe |
| **Container** | Running instance of an image | House built from blueprint |
| **Dockerfile** | Instructions to build an image | The recipe steps |
| **docker-compose** | Multi-container orchestration | Managing multiple recipes |
| **Volume** | Persistent data storage | External hard drive |

### Docker vs Virtual Machines
```
VM:      [App] → [Guest OS] → [Hypervisor] → [Host OS]
Docker:  [App] → [Container Runtime] → [Host OS]
```
Containers share the host OS kernel — much lighter than VMs.

> **Key Concept:** Docker doesn't virtualize hardware. It uses Linux namespaces and cgroups to isolate processes. This makes containers start in seconds (not minutes like VMs) and use far less memory.

steps.line:
# Dockerfile — Build instructions for your image
FROM python:3.12-slim

explanation.en: Start from the official Python 3.12 slim image — lightweight base
Set the working directory inside the container

steps.line:
# Copy requirements first for better caching
COPY requirements.txt .

explanation.en: Copy requirements.txt first — Docker caches this layer
Install dependencies — this layer is cached if requirements.txt hasn't changed

steps.line:
# Copy the rest of the application
COPY . .

explanation.en: Copy all app files into the container
Document which port the container listens on (informational)
Default command to run when the container starts

steps.line:
# .dockerignore — Exclude files from the build
__pycache__
*.pyc
.env
venv/
.git
*.db

explanation.en: Like .gitignore but for Docker — keeps the image small and clean

steps.line:
# Build the image
# $ docker build -t pymaster-app:latest .

explanation.en: Build an image named 'pymaster-app' tagged 'latest' from current directory

steps.line:
# Run the container
# $ docker run -d -p 8000:8000 --name myapp --env-file .env pymaster-app:latest

explanation.en: -d detached, -p maps host:container ports, --env-file loads secrets

steps.line:
# docker-compose.yml — Multi-service setup
version: '3.8'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    ports:
      - '8000:8000'
    env_file:
      - .env
    depends_on:
      - db
    volumes:
      - ./uploads:/app/uploads

explanation.en: Define the web service: build from Dockerfile, map port, load env, mount volume

steps.line:
db:
    image: postgres:16
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: myapp
      POSTGRES_USER: appuser
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secret
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - '5432:5432'

volumes:
  pgdata:

explanation.en: PostgreSQL service with named volume for persistent data

steps.line:
# $ docker-compose up -d        # Start all services
# $ docker-compose logs web     # View web logs
# $ docker-compose down          # Stop and remove containers
# $ docker-compose exec web bash # Shell into web container

explanation.en: Compose commands to manage the multi-container application

instruction.en: Write a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for a FastAPI app with a PostgreSQL database. The Dockerfile should use multi-stage build or proper caching. The compose file should define both services with appropriate environment variables and volumes.
instruction.ta: PostgreSQL தரவுத்தளத்துடன் FastAPI பயன்பாட்டிற்கான Dockerfile மற்றும் docker-compose.yml எழுதுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
# Dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY . .

EXPOSE 8000

CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000", "--workers", "4"]

# docker-compose.yml
# version: '3.8'
# services:
#   api:
#     build: .
#     ports:
#       - '8000:8000'
#     environment:
#       - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/myapp
#     depends_on:
#       - db
#   db:
#     image: postgres:16
#     environment:
#       POSTGRES_DB: myapp
#       POSTGRES_USER: user
#       POSTGRES_PASSWORD: pass
#     volumes:
#       - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
# volumes:
#   pgdata:
practice_challenges.expected_output: docker-compose up builds and starts both API and database containers

en: Copy requirements.txt before copying all files for better layer caching
Use --no-cache-dir with pip to keep the image small
depends_on ensures the database starts before the API
Named volumes persist data even when containers are removed
ta: சிறந்த அடுக்கு கேச்சிங்கிற்கு அனைத்து கோப்புகளையும் நகலெடுப்பதற்கு முன் requirements.txt-ஐ நகலெடுக்கவும்
depends_on API-க்கு முன் தரவுத்தளம் தொடங்குவதை உறுதி செய்கிறது
கொள்கலன்கள் நீக்கப்பட்டாலும் பெயரிடப்பட்ட தொகுதிகள் தரவைப் பாதுகாக்கின்றன

q.en: What is the difference between a Docker image and a container?

en: An image is a read-only template; a container is a running instance of an image
A container is read-only; an image is running
An image is for development; a container is for production
ta: படம் படிக்க மட்டும் வார்ப்புரு; கொள்கலன் படத்தின் இயங்கும் நிகழ்வு

q.en: Why should you COPY requirements.txt before COPY . . in a Dockerfile?
q.ta: Dockerfile-ல் COPY . . க்கு முன் COPY requirements.txt ஏன் செய்ய வேண்டும்?

en: Docker caches layers — if requirements haven't changed, pip install is skipped on rebuild
ta: Docker அடுக்குகளை கேச் செய்கிறது — requirements மாறவில்லை என்றால், மீண்டும் கட்டும்போது pip install தவிர்க்கப்படும்

en: Builds images and starts all services in detached (background) mode
ta: படங்களை உருவாக்கி அனைத்து சேவைகளையும் பின்னணி முறையில் தொடங்குகிறது
